annotations.label: Flat module

anchor.snippet: a module M over a ring R is flat if taking the tensor product over R with M preserves exact sequences

annotations.note: Module.Flat is defined via tensor product preserving injectivity of finitely generated submodule inclusions; the exact-sequence formulation is given by Module.Flat.iff_lTensor_exact.

annotations.label: Faithfully flat module

anchor.snippet: A module is faithfully flat if taking the tensor product with a sequence produces an exact sequence if and only if the original sequence is exact

annotations.note: Module.FaithfullyFlat is defined via flatness plus IM ≠ M for maximal ideals; equivalence with the exactness-iff characterization is Module.FaithfullyFlat.iff_exact_iff_lTensor_exact.

annotations.label: Flat module (injectivity definition)

anchor.snippet: A left module M over a ring R is flat if the following condition is satisfied

annotations.note: Module.Flat is defined exactly by injectivity of tensored inclusions of (finitely generated) submodules; Module.Flat.iff_rTensor_preserves_injective_linearMap gives the formulation in terms of arbitrary injective linear maps.

annotations.label: Restriction to finitely generated ideals

anchor.snippet: it is enough to restrict the injections

annotations.note: Module.Flat.iff_rTensor_injective states that flatness is equivalent to injectivity of I ⊗ M → R ⊗ M for finitely generated ideals I.

annotations.label: Equivalent definition via exact functor

anchor.snippet: an R -module M is flat if the tensor product with M is an exact functor

annotations.note: Module.Flat.iff_lTensor_exact (and iff_rTensor_exact) characterizes flatness as preservation of exactness by the tensor functor.

annotations.label: Equational characterization of flatness

anchor.snippet: Flatness can also be characterized by the following equational condition

annotations.note: Module.Flat.iff_forall_isTrivialRelation tagged with Stacks 00HK gives the equational criterion: every relation ∑ fᵢxᵢ = 0 in M is trivial.

annotations.label: Characterization via homomorphisms factoring through free modules

anchor.snippet: An R -module M is flat if and only if the following condition holds

annotations.note: Module.Flat.iff_forall_exists_factorization (Stacks 058D) characterizes flatness via factorization through finite free modules.

annotations.label: Relations to free, projective, torsion-free

anchor.snippet: every flat module is torsion-free , every projective module is flat, and every free module is projective

annotations.note: Module.Flat.of_projective, Module.Flat.of_free, and Module.Flat.isTorsionFree (Mathlib.RingTheory.Flat.TorsionFree) together cover all three implications.

annotations.label: Finitely generated flat iff locally free

anchor.snippet: a finitely generated module is flat if and only it is locally free

annotations.note: Mathlib has Module.free_of_flat_of_isLocalRing for finite+flat over local rings and freeLocus_eq_univ for finitely presented flat modules, but the precise iff with locally free for finitely generated is not stated as one decl.

annotations.label: Flat implies torsion-free

anchor.snippet: Every flat module is torsion-free

annotations.note: Module.Flat.isTorsionFree (an instance) shows every flat module is torsion-free; Module.Flat.torsion_eq_bot is the same in equational form.

annotations.label: Converse over PIDs and Dedekind rings

anchor.snippet: The converse holds over the integers, and more generally over principal ideal domains and Dedekind rings

annotations.note: IsDedekindDomain.flat_iff_torsion_eq_bot (Stacks 0AUW) and Module.Flat.flat_iff_torsion_eq_bot_of_isBezout cover Dedekind rings and PIDs/Bezout domains respectively.

annotations.label: Prüfer domain

anchor.snippet: An integral domain over which every torsion-free module is flat is called a Prüfer domain

annotations.note: Mathlib has a TODO comment in RingTheory/Flat/TorsionFree.lean noting that Prüfer domains have not yet been defined.

annotations.label: Characterization of projective modules

anchor.snippet: A module M is projective if and only if there is a free module G and two linear maps

annotations.note: Module.Projective.iff_split (and Module.projective_def') characterize projectivity as being a retract/direct summand of a free module.

annotations.label: Projective implies flat

anchor.snippet: Every projective module is flat

annotations.note: Module.Flat.of_projective is a typeclass instance deriving Flat from Projective.

annotations.label: Finitely presented flat is projective

anchor.snippet: A finitely presented module

annotations.note: Module.Flat.projective_of_finitePresentation tagged with Stacks 00NX states that a finitely presented flat module is projective.

annotations.label: Finitely generated flat is projective over Noetherian rings

anchor.snippet: Over a Noetherian ring , every finitely generated flat module is projective

annotations.note: Mathlib has projective_of_finitePresentation; combined with Module.Finite ⇒ FinitePresentation over Noetherian, this yields the statement, but there is no dedicated decl spelling out the Noetherian+finite flat case.

annotations.label: Finitely generated flat is free over local rings

anchor.snippet: On a local ring every finitely generated flat module is free

annotations.note: Module.free_of_flat_of_isLocalRing proves Module.Finite + Module.Flat over a local ring implies Module.Free.

annotations.label: Finitely generated flat non-projective module

anchor.snippet: A finitely generated flat module that is not projective can be built as follows

annotations.note: No corresponding non-example construction was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: R/I generally not flat

anchor.snippet: If I is an ideal in a Noetherian commutative ring R

annotations.note: No general statement that R/I fails to be flat unless I is generated by an idempotent was located in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Torsion modules over Z not flat

anchor.snippet: a module that contains nonzero torsion elements is not flat

annotations.note: Mathlib's Module.Flat.isTorsionFree implies (contrapositively) any nonzero-torsion module is not flat, but this is not stated as a named non-example.

annotations.label: Direct sum flatness

anchor.snippet: The direct sum

annotations.note: Module.Flat.directSum_iff (and the directSum instance) state that a direct sum is flat iff each summand is.

annotations.label: Direct limit of flat modules is flat

anchor.snippet: The direct limit of flat modules is flat

annotations.note: No statement that filtered/direct limits of flat modules are flat was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Direct product flat iff coherent ring

anchor.snippet: Direct products of flat modules need not in general be flat

annotations.note: Coherent rings and the corresponding characterization via direct products of flat modules are not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Flat ring homomorphism

anchor.snippet: A ring homomorphism

annotations.note: RingHom.Flat is defined as flatness of S as an R-module via the homomorphism f.

annotations.label: Localization is flat

anchor.snippet: the localization

annotations.note: IsLocalization.flat shows any localization is flat over the base ring.

annotations.label: Completion is flat

anchor.snippet: the completion

annotations.note: AdicCompletion.flat_of_isNoetherian shows the I-adic completion is flat when R is Noetherian, matching the standard statement; the more general completion contexts aren't generalized.

annotations.label: Flatness is a local property

anchor.snippet: If M is an R -module the three following conditions are equivalent

annotations.note: Module.flat_of_isLocalized_maximal and Module.flat_of_isLocalized_span (with Module.flat_iff_of_isLocalization) establish that flatness can be checked locally at maximal ideals.

annotations.label: Flat morphism of schemes

anchor.snippet: A morphism

annotations.note: AlgebraicGeometry.Flat is the predicate that a morphism of schemes is flat, defined via flatness of the ring maps Γ(Y,U) → Γ(X,V).

annotations.label: Flat extension and primitive polynomial

anchor.snippet: Let

annotations.note: This specific example illustrating a flat extension built from a primitive polynomial does not appear in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Faithfully flat algebra

anchor.snippet: A module is faithfully flat if taking the tensor product with a sequence produces an exact sequence if and only if the original sequence is exact

annotations.note: Module.FaithfullyFlat applies to algebras viewed as modules; Mathlib.RingTheory.Flat.FaithfullyFlat.Algebra develops the algebra setting.

annotations.label: Equivalent conditions for faithful flatness

anchor.snippet: the following conditions are equivalent

annotations.note: Multiple iff lemmas (iff_flat_and_proper_ideal, iff_flat_and_rTensor_faithful, iff_exact_iff_rTensor_exact, iff_zero_iff_lTensor_zero) cover the standard equivalent characterizations.

annotations.label: Flat local homomorphism is faithfully flat

anchor.snippet: The second condition implies that a flat local homomorphism of local rings is faithfully flat

annotations.note: Module.FaithfullyFlat.of_flat_of_isLocalHom states that flat + local homomorphism of local rings implies faithfully flat.

annotations.label: Submersive property

anchor.snippet: The second-last condition can be stated in the following strengthened form

annotations.note: Mathlib has the surjectivity of Spec under faithfully flat maps (comap_surjective_of_faithfullyFlat), but not the full submersive/quotient-map topological strengthening.

annotations.label: Free implies faithfully flat

anchor.snippet: is a nonzero free R -module is faithfully flat

annotations.note: An anonymous instance `[Nontrivial M] [Module.Free R M] : FaithfullyFlat R M` proves nonzero free modules are faithfully flat.

annotations.label: Polynomial ring is faithfully flat

anchor.snippet: A polynomial ring is a faithfully flat extension of its ring of coefficients

annotations.note: R[X] is a free R-module in Mathlib so FaithfullyFlat R R[X] is inferrable via the free-implies-faithfully-flat instance, but no named declaration spells this out.

annotations.label: Monic polynomial inclusion is faithfully flat

anchor.snippet: the inclusion

annotations.note: The faithfully flat extension R → R[X]/(monic) is not formalized as a named instance in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Direct product of localizations faithfully flat

anchor.snippet: The direct product

annotations.note: No statement asserting the product of localizations at all maximal ideals is faithfully flat was located.

annotations.label: Direct sum of localizations at all primes

anchor.snippet: The direct sum of the localizations

annotations.note: No statement about faithful flatness of the direct sum of localizations at all primes was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Amitsur complex exactness

anchor.snippet: there is an associated complex called the Amitsur complex

annotations.note: The Amitsur complex is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Theorem of transition characterization

anchor.snippet: Here is one characterization of a faithfully flat homomorphism for a not-necessarily-flat homomorphism

annotations.note: The characterization of faithfully flat local homomorphisms via transition is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Flatness via vanishing Tor

anchor.snippet: Flatness may also be expressed using the Tor functors

annotations.note: Mathlib has CategoryTheory.Tor as a left-derived functor of tensor, but no theorem characterizing Module.Flat via vanishing of Tor.

annotations.label: Vanishing of first Tor suffices

anchor.snippet: it is enough to check that the first Tor term vanishes

annotations.note: No statement that vanishing of Tor₁ alone implies flatness has been formalized.

annotations.label: Flatness in short exact sequences

anchor.snippet: one can then easily prove facts about a short exact sequence

annotations.note: Long exact Tor sequence and the standard "two of three flat ⇒ third flat" consequences are not formalized for Module.Flat.

annotations.label: Flat resolution

anchor.snippet: A flat resolution of a module

annotations.note: The notion of a flat resolution is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Flat dimension

anchor.snippet: the minimal length among all finite flat resolutions

annotations.note: Flat (Tor-)dimension of a module is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Module with vanishing Tor is flat

anchor.snippet: As an example, consider a module

annotations.note: No example tying vanishing Tor on resolutions to flatness is formalized.

annotations.label: Flat cover

anchor.snippet: every module M would be the epimorphic image of a flat module F

annotations.note: Flat covers/precovers are not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Flat cover conjecture resolved

anchor.snippet: The conjecture turned out to be true, resolved positively and proved simultaneously by L. Bican, R. El Bashir and E. Enochs

annotations.note: The Bican–El Bashir–Enochs flat cover theorem is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Free modules are flat constructively

anchor.snippet: no choice is needed to prove that free modules are flat

annotations.note: Module.Flat.of_free (and Module.Flat.finsupp) prove free modules are flat; Mathlib does not separately track constructive content.
